introduction: Welcome to the ENG192 Robotics Lab! I'm Jackie, and I'll be your guide today. We'll make five stops around the lab. Follow me!
conclusion: That concludes our lab tour! I hope you enjoyed seeing what we do here. Feel free to ask me any questions — just head back to the home screen and tap Ask Jackie!

stops.name: Open Lab Area
stops.narration: We're starting here in the open lab area. This is the main floor of ENG192 where students gather, collaborate, and run experiments. It's a flexible space — lectures happen here, demos happen here, and most of the hands-on robotics learning starts right in this room.

stops.name: Industrial Robots
stops.narration: Next up, the industrial robots station. Over here we have our SCARA robot and other industrial arms used for teaching automation, pick-and-place tasks, and motion programming. Students learn how real factory-grade robots are controlled, how to plan trajectories, and how to integrate grippers and sensors.

stops.name: Project Table
stops.narration: This is the project table. It's exactly what it sounds like — a dedicated workspace where students sit down and build. Capstone teams, class projects, and research prototypes all come together right here.

stops.name: Research Robots
stops.narration: Here are our research robots — the Franka Emika and the Aubo arm. These are collaborative robots used for advanced research in manipulation, human-robot interaction, and AI-driven control. Graduate students use these platforms for thesis work on things like learning from demonstration and safe interaction.

stops.name: Fabrication Room
stops.narration: Our last stop is the fabrication room. This is where our 3D printers live. Students prototype custom robot parts, sensor mounts, and mechanical fixtures here.
